summary: A catastrophic magnitude 7.8 earthquake struck the southern Philippines on June 8, killing at least 31 people. The death toll rose to 37 by June 9 as aftershocks continued, and by June 14 the quake was reported to have raised the seabed by two metres, causing widespread marine die-off. In the Middle East, a complex and rapidly evolving conflict unfolded. A ceasefire between Iran and Israel collapsed on June 8, and strikes were exchanged. After a U.S. helicopter was downed, the United States and Iran traded strikes on June 10 and continued on June 11, with a U.S. strike on a tanker off Oman killing three Indian seafarers. On June 12, Iran published a draft deal with the U.S. for the release of $24 billion in assets. Both the U.S. and Iran indicated on June 13 that a ceasefire deal was close but final details remained. Separately, Israel launched new strikes in southern Lebanon on June 9. In Asia, Pakistan launched deadly airstrikes on three Afghan provinces on June 10. On June 12, the Taliban arrested 30 women for hijab violations as a protest turned deadly. In India, the Constitutional Amendment Bill for women's quota was defeated in Parliament on June 14. A joint operation by Venezuela and the U.S. on June 13 killed the leader of the Tren de Aragua gang. The Justice Department approved a $110 billion merger between Paramount and Warner on June 13. Elon Musk became the world's first trillionaire after SpaceX stock debuted on the same day. On June 14, UK forces intercepted a tanker from Russia's shadow fleet in the English Channel. In Peru, Keiko Fujimori won the presidency on June 12. The Pentagon blacklisted Alibaba, Baidu, and BYD over Chinese military ties on June 9. Stories that appeared only briefly included the initial Philippines earthquake report, Xi Jinping's North Korea visit, and the Iran-Israel exchange, which quickly escalated into the broader U.S.-Iran confrontation. The Armenian election and a major IPO in Japan also faded from the headlines after single-day appearances.
fadedStories: Magnitude 7.8 earthquake kills at least 31 in southern Philippines

South Africa unveils comprehensive plan to tackle illegal immigration
Philippines earthquake death toll rises to 37 as aftershocks continue
Pentagon blacklists Alibaba, Baidu, BYD over Chinese military ties
